title: Airline stocks dive in wake of Russia's invasion of Ukraine
text: Shares of air carriers took a deep dive in premarket trading Thursday, as Russia's invasion of Ukraine weighed heavily on travel-related stocks. The U.S. Global Jets ETF JETS, -3.21% sank 5.1% toward a two-month low, as futures ES00, -2.47% for the S&P 500 SPX, -1.84% slumped 2.4% toward a nine-month low.

title: Fuel Prices To Weigh On United Airlines Stock In The Near Term
text: The shares of United Airlines observed a strong rally in recent weeks assisted by pent-up travel demand and declining coronavirus infections. However, the benchmark oil prices have surged by 26% from $75/bbl in early January to $95/bbl at present – weighing on the bottom line of all transportation.

title: U.S. airlines see 2021 traffic jump, but below pre-pandemic levels
text: U.S. passenger airlines carried 670.4 million passengers in 2021, up 83% over 2020 levels but still down significantly from pre-pandemic levels, the Transportation Department said on Monday.
